ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON AUG-31-2010 IN DEPT. 220, MATTER WAS HEARD AND ARGURED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULINGS: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A) AND (D). TRIAL IS SET AT 11:15AM ON NOV. 29, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 CONDITIONED ON PLAINTIFFS' AGREEMENT THAT PARTIES STIPULATED TO GO. NO. 140. SUMMARY MOTIONS MAY BE HEARD ON 15 DAYS NOTICE WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR E-SERVICE AND MAY BE HEARD UP TO AND INCLUDING NOVEMBER 23. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET IN FOR NOVEMBER 19, 2010 AT 9:00 AM. IN DEPT. 603. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR SEPTEMBER 21, 2010 AT 1:30 PM. IN DEPT. 220. NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 15, 2010 PLAINTIFFS TO MAKE ALL SETTLEMENT DEMANDS ON ALL DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFFS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND IDENTIFICATION WITNESSES WITH IN 21 DAYS AND DEFENDANT SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES WITHIN 35 DAYS. PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS SHALL BE SERVED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL. PLAINTIFFS SHALL WITHIN 3 DAYS DISCLOSE THE NAMES OF ANY BANKRUPTCY TRUST CLAIM SUBMITTED. DISCOVERY SHALL REMAIN OPEN UNTIL DATE OF TRIAL. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING A PREFERENCE/SCHEDULING ORDER. PENDING WRITTEN ORDER. JUDGE HAROLD KAHN, CLERK S. Off Calendar May-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
L. Judiee Tran of SNR DENTON (415-882-03210), appearing for defendant / moving party Rapid-American Corporation
Laurel Halbany of WATERS KRAUSE & PAUL (800-226-9880), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing party Clinton Bogan Jr., et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT RAPID-AMERICAN CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rapid-American Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden under the factually devoid prong and the negation of an element prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bogan was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Weiner's declaration are sustained as to paragraphs 4 and 6 and overruled as to paragraph 5. Plaintiff's objections to the purported Carey-Canadian Mines Ltd. sales records and authenticating affidavit are sustained. Defendant's objections to Mr. Lehnert's deposition testimony are sustained. Even if Mr. Lehnert's deposition testimony were admissible, they do not create a triable issue whether the Bestwall joint compound to which Mr. Bogan was exposed in 1963 contained asbestos fiber attributable to defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)
